def: adj. Quadruple, fourfold.
n. f. Playing chaugān .
s. f. A straight ḥuḳḳa -snake or tube for smoking through.
s. m. A rabbit, hare.
n. m. An earthen toy, consisting of four small cups joined together. chau-ghaṛā ilāchī , n. f. The best kind of small cardamom.
more: s. m. A small box of gold or silver, with four partitions, for holding perfumes.

def: adv. On all sides. s. Neighbourhood, district.
1. adj. See چوکونا chaukonā. 2. A kind of oblong tray.
s. m. A turkī horse, a horse with slit ears.
n. m. A caste of Rājpūts , from चतुर्वाहु Chaturvāhu their first king.
(چو Four, and हट्ट Market) s. m. A market where four roads meet.
= 3 × 20+10+4. adj. S. चतुःसप्तति Seventyfour.
s.m. A scrambler.
s. f. Attention, circumspection, watchfulness, alertness, caution.
n. f. S. चतुष्काष्ट lit . four posts. 1. A door frame. 2. Threshold; entrance.
n. m. 1. A frame of a picture, etc. 2. ( Arch .) The basement of a building.

n. 1. A quadrilateral. 2. The four quarters; the world. chau-khūṅṭā, chau-khūṅṭī , adj. Quadrangular. chau-gunā, chaug'nā , adj. S. चतुर्गुण Four times; four fold. chau-goshī, navābī , n. f. 1. A four-sided native cap. 2. An animal whose ears are slit, particularly a horse. chau-lāvā , n. m. A large well with water enough for four lāos , q. v. be-brābar chau-khūṅṭ ; A. mukhtalif-ul-azlā . A trapezoid.
more: n. m. S. चटुष्कोण 1. A four-sided figure. 2. The world. chau-khūṅṭ , adv. On all four-sides; all round. chau-khūṅṭā, chau-khūṅṭ, chau-konyā , adj. 1. Four-cornered; quadrangular. 2. ( Bot .) Lozenge-shaped; rhomboidalia. chau-khūṅṭ-barābar-bāzū , S. विषमकोण चतुर्भुज ( Math .) n. m. A rhombus ( moaiyan ). chau-khūṅṭ do bīch-barābar , S. बिषम चतुर्भुज A. zū-zulfā . ( Math .) n. m. A trapezium. chau-khūṅṭ do barābar bāzū , S. बिमम कोण आयत ( Math .) A rhomboid ( shabah ba-moaiyan ). chau-khūṅt bīch-barābar , S. समानांतर चतुर्भुज ( Math .) A parallelogram (A. mutavāzī-ul-azlāٴa ).

def: adj. Square.

n. m. A watchman; sentinel; guard. chaukī-dār-i-dehātī , A village watchman. chaukī-dār ke bhed meṅ , With the privity of the watchman. chaukī-dāroṅ kā bakhshī , One who collects the chaukīdārī , q. v.
W. n. f. 1. The office of a watchman. 2. The tax on account of watch and ward. 3. The fees or wages paid to the town or village watchman.
v.n. To scramble.
adj. Four-cornered, square.
n. m. chaukṛī , n. f. A congregate of four. Bheliyoṅ kā chaukṛā. chaukṛāt' , n. f. An assembly of four; hence, a council of arbitration.
s.f. = ćǒhal, q.v.
s. f. A kind of vegetable. (Amaranthus polygamus).
s. m. A necklace of four strings.
more: s. m. Necklace of four rows of small round gold beads threaded on silk ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

def: s. f. A necklace of four strings.
more: s. f. Necklace of four rows of small round gold beads threaded on silk ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

def: adj. & s.m. = ćǒhalā, ćǒhlī, qq.v.

n. m. S. चातुर्मास, Pālī, chatumasam . 1. The four months of the rainy season ( barkhā ). 2. Lands ploughed and left fallow during the chau-māsā and prepared for the rabī sowing. 3. A kind of song sung during the rains descriptive of the season. chau-māsiyā , n. m. A ploughman hired for the rainy season. chau-mahlā , n. m. A four-storied (house, etc.). chau-mag/?/zā , n. m. 1. A walnut ( akhroṭ ). 2. A person with a large head.
s. m. 1. A lamp-stand with partitions or burners. 2. A name of Brahmā.
s. f. 1. A name of the goddess Devī. 2. The seed of a tree, called रुद्राक्ष (Scævola lobelia or Elæocarpus ganitrus).

adj. S. चतुर्+मुख Pālī, chatummukho . Having four faces. chau-mukkhā dīyā , n. m. A lamp with four wicks. chau-mukhā, yā chau-bāī, laṛnā , v. n. To be good at argument. chau-mekhā , n. m. 1. The act of tying the four legs of a horse. 2. A punishment which consists in tying a man's arms and legs. chau'pā , n. m. S. चतुष्पाद ; P. chaupayā . A quadruped; cattle; beast of burden. Ghāṅs thuṛeṛī, chaunpe bohte; Sagar baḍe, na ko lhauṛā . Rus. song. 'So little grass, so many kine, And all full grown, no small kine mine.' chau-haṭṭā , n. m. A square surrounded by shops.
more: 1. adj. Having four burners, &c. (a lamp). 2. s. m. A lamp-stand with four partitions. 3. A kind of پٿا paṭā, q. v.

ety: Prk. छावडओ; S. शाव+ल or र+कः
def: s.m. The young of any animal; — (dialec.) boy, son ( = beṭā).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀙𑀼𑀯𑀇 (chuvaï). Cognate with Punjabi ਛੂਹਣਾ (chūhṇā) / چھوہنا (chūhṇā).

def: (dakh.) On the four sides: see چودهير chau-dhīr, under چو chau.
s.f. = ćhāʼonī, q.v. s.f. Thatching, roofing, covering; the art of thatching; — huts or barracks for soldiers, a cantonment
subs: 'To thatch a roof'; to make a home for oneself, to take up (one's) abode
To form or make a cantonment
(-meṅ), To canton, to take up quarters (in).

def: s.m. Seasoning, spices with which food is seasoned.
more: s. m. Spices with which food is seasoned.

def: v.t. To throw in seasoning, to season
more: v. a. To fry with a seasoning of spices ( baghārnā ).

def: adj. See چوسٿهـ.
s. m. A kind of sedan. See چوپالا chau-pālā.
s. f. A sort of metre or verse consisting of four ( पद ) lines.
A verse of four lines, each line of 8 + 7 = 15 instants. The final syllable must be short. Kellogg .
A verse of four lines, each line of 10 + 8 + 12 = 30 instants. The last foot is a spondee. The subordinate rhymes fall at the 10th and 18th instant. Kellogg .
adj. (dakh.) Fifty four: see چون chauwan.
n. f. S. चतुष्पुटी See پچيسي The cloth or board on which the game is played, having two transverse bars in the form of a cross. chaupaṛ kā bāzār , n. m. Two transverse roads or lanes.
more: Hin. Wom. n. m. S. सर्पि butter. Oil; butter; grease. Do paisā bhar chopaṛ mile to dehī khaṛī rahe .

def: adj. lit . four doors. 1. Wide open; open. Chaupaṭ paṭ khol jo soyā, Ek din khādā khā-kar royā . Rus. 2. Blind; ignorant. 3. Razed to the ground; ruined. Sārā shahar chaupaṭ paṛā hai . 4. Ruined; brought to poverty. chaupaṭ k. , v. a. To raze to the ground ( ujāṛnā , 3, 7.)
s. f. A small tract, an essay, a pamphlet, a pocketbook.
s. (dakh.) See چوپڙ chaupaṛ.
s.m. A large stack of jawār, or bājrā, &c. collected for fodder; — walking a boundary with a raw cow-skin on the head under a solemn oath to decide correctly.
s.m. Shaving of the head or beard; — the office of barber
adj. Abandoned, vitious, damaged.
s. f. (dakh.) See چوڙاٴي chauṛā-ī.

Tir. n. m. Junction or crossing of four roads; cross-roads. chau-rāhe kī ṭhīkrī , ( Slang .) n. f. A prostitute. chau-rāhe meṅ , adv. On the public road; in public. chau-kor , n. m. A square. chau-kor lohā , n. m. Bar iron.
(چو Four, and راه Way) s. m. A cross-road.
s. f. 1. Breadth, extent, width, extension. 2. Boasting. چوڙاٴي مارنا يا کرنا chauṛā-ī-mārnā or -karnā, To boast, to give oneʼs self airs.

n. f. Breadth; width; dilatation. chauṛe meṅ, din dahāṛe, khule /?/khazāne , adv. In the open; in broad daylight ( din dhaulī ). chauṛe meṅ baiṭhā rah jānā , v. n. To be abandoned, ruined. chauṛe meṅ luṭ jānā , v. n. To be robbed on the highway in broad daylight.
adj. S. चतुर्नवति Ninety-four.
s. f. Equality of surface, evenness, levelness.
s.f. A stack or bundle of fodder.
adj. (dakh.) Ninety four: see چورانوي chaurānawwe.
adj. Sixty four.
= 3 × 20 + 4 adj. S. चतुःषष्टि, Pālī, chatusaṭṭhī . Sixty-four. chausaṭh ghaṛī , n. f. 64 gharis or 24 hours; all day and night ( āṭh pahar ); always.

adj. Of four folds. chau-tahī, chau-taī , n. f. A piece of cloth of two breadths of double length. chau-haddā, chau-gaḍḍā, chau-khanḍī, chau-sīmāṅ, chau-sivānā, chomṭā , n. m. A raised mound indicating where the boundaries of four villages meet. chau-haddī, chau-sīmā ; A. hudūd arbā , n. f. 1. The four boundaries; environs; surrounding country. 2. Neighbourhood; vicinage.
s. m. A mode in musick.
adj. (dakh.) Forty four: see چواليس chawālīs.
s. m. A four-stringed musical instrument.
s. m. See چبوتره chabūtara.
s. f. The fourth part, a quarter.
adv. In the fourth place; fourthly. chauthe chandarmā . The fourth moon, considered unlucky.
s. f. A tempest, hurricane, commotion. (q. d. wind blowing from four quarters).
more: or from چواو chawāʼo ) s. m. Reporter, backbiter.

def: s.m. The young of any animal; a young elephant (from ten to twenty years old) = ćhaunā.
s.f. Shade, &c. ( = ćhāṅh, q.v.). s.f. Shade, shadow; a reflected image, reflection
s.m. A thatcher.
adj. Sixth ( = ćhaṭhā, q.v.). adj. (f. -ī), Sixth ( = ćhaṭā, q.v.). s.m. An umbrella; — a broad and prominent chest (like that of an athlete), the breast; — a mushroom, a fungus; — mouldiness.
s.f. A kind of basket (made of twigs of the jhāʼū or tamarisk tree; syn. ćhabṛā).
s.f. Hide, skin; colour; light, brilliance, lustre, splendour; beauty (see ćhab).
more: n. m. 1. Love; affection. 2. Tenderness; pity. 3. (S. क्षोभ to be angry) Anger ( gussā ). chho honā , v. n. To be angry. Chho ho gaī, sās khasam-roī . Rus. Wom. chho'hī , adj. Angry.

n. f. Blowing after a prayer or charm. chhū chhū banānā , v. a. To make a fool of. chhū chhū bannā , v. n. To be off; go away. chhū mantar , A charm; incantation; Hey presto. chhū yā chhū mantar k. , To pronounce a charm; to conjure; cast a spell. chhū honā, uṛān chhū h. , v. n. To vanish; disappear; scamper.
s. (dakh. i. q. دعاپڙهکردم کرنا) Blowing on repeating a prayer (?).

def: s.m. Young (of an animal), young one, young creature; young tiger (cf. ćhāʼonā).
s. f. Name of a place in Dihlī.

ety: Prk. छयं or छइअं; S. क्षयः, rt. क्षि 'to destroy'
def: s.f. Loss, waste, diminution, destruction, decay, wasting or wearing away; removal; frailty; mortality; pulmonary consumption, any wasting disease
more: intj. S. छि Fy! foh! pooh! tush. chhī chhī , n. f. A lower class of Eurasians by whom this expression is commonly used.

n. f. H. chhānā to thatch. 1. A thatched house on a boat. 2. A pad for horses or oxen. 3. Offspring; children. Ab kī chhaī kī nirālī bāteṅ . Prov. The conversation of the rising generation is very strange. chhaī palān , n. m. A pack-saddle with the pad.
s. f. Mortality, frailty, destruction. چهي روگ क्षयरोग chhai-rog (vulg. chhaʼī-rog ) Consumption.
subs: adj. Causing or promoting decay or destruction, &c.; destructive.

def: s.f. Shade, shadow; a shady place; reflected image, reflection; light, lustre, splendour; colour; — darkness, obscurity; — s.m.(?) An apparition, a spectre; a faint image or resemblance
more: n. f. S. छाया, Pr. chhāhā , P. sāyā . 1. Shade ( chhāṅ ). 1. Chhāvā baṛī māyā saë . Rus. Prov. A shade (or roof) over your head is a great blessing. 2. Jit chhāvā dekhe rūkhāṅ kī, Chādar uṭhāṅ bichhā jākar, Lalvā, tauṅ ḍhor charā jākar! Rus. song. Where'er you see, boy, shady trees, There spread your cloth and lie at ease, There let the cattle 'bout you graze. 2. ( Ast .) Umbra . 3. An apparition; a spectre; an evil spirit. 4. Refuge; protection; defence; asylum. chhāyā-dān , n. m. Offering up one's shadow. ☞ To ward off the influence of an evil star one must see his face in a cup of oil which is then given to the ḍakaut , q. v.
s. m. 1. Apparition, spectre. 2. Shade. 3. ( met. ) Somewhat resembling. 4. A mode in musick.
subs: asylum, protection ( = sāya); — a mode (in music)
s.m. Ascertaining the time by observing the shadow (of an object)
s.m. Ether, the atmosphere or firmament
s.m. A large umbrageous tree (giving abundant shade or shelter)
s.m. Offering up one's shadow (to ward off the influence of evil: this is effected by looking at one's image in a cup of oil, which is then presented to the the ḍakaut, q.v.)
s.m. 'Friend of the shade'; a parasol or umbrella
ćhāyā-vant, adj. Possessing or granting shade, shady, shadowy

def: s.f. = ćhāyā, q.v. s.f. Shade, shadow; a shady place; reflected image, reflection; light, lustre, splendour; colour; — darkness, obscurity; — s.m.(?) An apparition, a spectre; a faint image or resemblance
subs: asylum, protection ( = sāya); — a mode (in music)
s.m. Ascertaining the time by observing the shadow (of an object)
s.m. Ether, the atmosphere or firmament
s.m. A large umbrageous tree (giving abundant shade or shelter)
s.m. Offering up one's shadow (to ward off the influence of evil: this is effected by looking at one's image in a cup of oil, which is then presented to the the ḍakaut, q.v.)
s.m. 'Friend of the shade'; a parasol or umbrella
ćhāyā-vant, adj. Possessing or granting shade, shady, shadowy

ety: S. क्षय+कः, rt. क्षि 'to dwell,' &c.; cf. also ćhāwā; rt. छद्
def: s.m. (f. ćhaʼī), Race, family, generation; — child, boy (e.g. ab-kī ćhaʼī kī nirālī bāteṅ, 'The language or conversation of the rising generation is extraordinary').

ety: S. क्षययितव्य+कंः, or क्षेय+कः, rt. क्षि 'to destroy'
def: s.m. A victim.

s.f.(m.?) = ćhāyā, ćhāṅ, q.v. s.f. Shade, shadow; a shady place; reflected image, reflection; light, lustre, splendour; colour; — darkness, obscurity; — s.m.(?) An apparition, a spectre; a faint image or resemblance
subs: asylum, protection ( = sāya); — a mode (in music)
s.m. Ascertaining the time by observing the shadow (of an object)
s.m. Ether, the atmosphere or firmament
s.m. A large umbrageous tree (giving abundant shade or shelter)
s.m. Offering up one's shadow (to ward off the influence of evil: this is effected by looking at one's image in a cup of oil, which is then presented to the the ḍakaut, q.v.)
s.m. 'Friend of the shade'; a parasol or umbrella
ćhāyā-vant, adj. Possessing or granting shade, shady, shadowy

def: s.m. Name of a rāg or musical mode.
more: s. m. Name of a Rāg or musical mode.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Shady, umbrageous, shadowy.
adj. (dialec.) = ćha, q.v. s.m. The letter or sound ćha.

ety: Prk. छिद्दं; S. छिद्रं, rt. छिद्
def: s.m. A hole, opening, orifice, aperture (cf. next).
more: n. m. S. छिद्, Pr. chhindaī he cuts. Pālī. chhiddam . A hole ( sūrākh ); an aperture; opening. Nādī vādī bahu mile, karat kaleje chhed, Koī takhat tale kā na milā jā se pūchhūṅ bhed . Kabīr. Of disputants no end there are, my breast's all pierced throughout, No friend who can the mysteries of being tell about. chhed meṅ ho-kar havā kā nikalnā . Effusion of gases.
s. m. A hole, an orifice.

def: s.m. Cutting, cutting off, dividing; (in Math.) divisor, the denominator of a fraction; — a cutting, cut, section, slice, piece; — an incision, cleft, slit; — destruction, dissipation, interruption, vanishing, cessation, deprivation, want, deficiency.
s.m. A small insect of the weevil kind (destructive to corn), Calandra granaria; — the disease in corn occasioned by this insect.
s.m. Anything with which a hole is bored, borer, piercer, splitter.
s.m. Cutting, cutting or lopping off, removing; amputation, mutilation, tearing asunder, splitting; — boring, piercing (e.g. kan-ćhedān, 'ear-piercing'); — a hole, opening, orifice
more: n. m. ( In comp .) Boring; punching. Iskā kan-chhedan kab hogā?
subs: v.t. To cut, pare, lop off, &c.

ety: ćhed˚ = Prk. छिद्द(इ) or छिद्दे(इ) = S. छिद्रय(ति), fr. छिद्, rt. छिद्
def: v.t. To make a hole in, to bore, pierce, perforate; to cut, pare, cut or lop off
more: v. a. See bīṅdhnā . To make a hole; perforate. 1. Jis hāṅḍī meṅ khāeṅ, usī meṅ chhed kareṅ! Prov. He makes a hole in his own dish? 2. Sui kahe 'maiṅ chhedūṅ chhedūṅ pahle chhed karāë .' Prov. The needle says 'I'll pierce' 'I'll pierce,' being first pierced myself. (The wrongs we have suffered we pay back with interest).
v. a. To pierce, bore, perforate, penetrate.
subs: v.t. (intens.) To pierce through and through, &c.

def: adv. (Old H.), In (or at) the end, finally, at length.
satisfy. Cheeping cattle to drink when they are taken to the water.
n. m. The fortieth day of mourning ( chālīsvāṅ ). Mah.
more: adj. Fortieth, the fortieth day (of mourning, &c.).

def: v. n. To mewl; moan; cry with pain (a child).

n. m. 1. Face; countenance; visage. 1. Chehre pe thūk dūṅgā . 2. Chehrah bigāṛ dūṅgā . 2. A portrait; miniature. 3. A visor; mask. chehrah-bandī, chehrah-navīsī , n. f. A descriptive roll. chehreh-shāhī rupayā yā sikkā , Current money or coin, so called from the Queen's head. chehreh kī lenā , v. n. To make faces; mock. Kyā chehre kī lī hai. chehrah likhnā , v. a. 1. To make a descriptive roll; to enter a description (of a person). 2. To register; enroll. chehrah mohrah , n. m. Features; lineaments. Chehre mohre se durust hai. chehrah honā , v. n. To be registered or enlisted.
more: s. m. 1. Face, visage, countenance, air. 2. Description-roll. چـﮩره لکهنا chihra likhnā, To write a description-roll (soldierʼs). چـﮩره لکهانا chihra likhānā, 1. To enlist. 2. To take service and horses.

def: v.n. See ćhenā or ćheʼonā.
s.m. = ćhed, q.v. s.m. A hole, opening, orifice, aperture (cf. next).
more: n. m. S. छेदन cutting. A hole. See chhed .
s. m. (dakh.) Sneeze: see چهينک chhīnk.

def: adj. Tame, domesticated; shrewd, sharp, smart
subs: (ćheka+uktī), s.f. Indirect speech, insinuation, double-entendre, hint, &c.

def: s.f. = ćheṅk, q.v. s.f. Detention, stoppage, interruption, restraint, arrest; sequestration, confiscation.
s.m. A hole, &c. ( = ćhek, ćhed q.v.). s.m. A hole, opening, orifice, aperture (cf. next).

v.t. = ćheṅknā, q.v. v.t. To interrupt, to interfere with, cross the path of; to detain, prevent, stop, retain, bar, restrain, arrest; — to cut out, to cross or score out, to cancel; — to surpass, excel
more: v. a. 1. To come between (اوَنا 1.). 2. To cross one's path; stop one on the road; hinder one's progress. 3. To cut out; draw the pen across; erase. 4. To cancel; annul; nullify. 5. To surpass; excel ( jītnā , 2).
subs: v.t. (intens.) To stop, &c.; to sequester, to confiscate.

def: s.f. See s.v. ćhek. s.m. A hole, opening, orifice, aperture (cf. next).
s.m. (dialec.) A goat ( = ćherā, q.v.). s.m. A goat (see ćherī).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀙𑀯𑀺 (chavi) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀮𑁆𑀮- (-lla-), from Sanskrit छवी (chavī́). Cognate with Odia ଛଇଳା (chaiḷā), Punjabi ਛੈਲਾ /چھَیلا (chailā).

def: s.f. A she-goat ( = ćherī, q.v.t. s.f. A she-goat.
more: n. f. S. छागल A she-goat ( bakrī ).

def: s.m. The medicinal plant Vernonia (or Conyza) anthelmintica.
s.f. Welfare, happiness (see kshem)
more: s. f. Welfare. چهيم کشل क्षेमकुशल chh̤em-kushal, s. f. Welfare.

def: s.m. A fatherless child, an orphan.
s.f. The Brāhmaṇī kite or Coromandel eagle (regarded as a bird of good omen), Falco ponticerianus.
more: s. f. A sort of kite. (Falco ponticerianus).

def: v. n. To chatter, chirp, murmur, grumble, squeak.

Tir. n. f. A small ant. 1. Terī chīṅtī chīṅtī, terā bāl bāl! Children. 2. Chīṅṭī mar gaī . You have killed an ant. Said to a child who has had a fall. (Get up quick, its mother will bite you.) 3. Chūṅṭī kī āvāz arsh par . Prov. The cry of the ant (the poor) riseth up to Heaven. chiūṅṭiyāṅ lagnā , v. n. To prick. Dhūp ke māre badan meṅ chiūṅṭiyāṅ sī lagtī haiṅ . The sun feels so hot as though a swarm of ants crept over one's body.
v.t. = ćheʼonā, q.v.
s.m. Curd; a sweetmeat made of curds ( = ćhenā-barā).
s.m. A small kind of cymbal.
v.n. To be torn, be slit, be rent, be cleft or riven (as the eye of a needle, &c.), to be worn out, to be rubbed or worn away, to fray, to fret (as cloth); to become bad, to turn (as milk)
s.f. (prov.) = ćhināl, q.v. s.f. A harlot, a prostitute, a wanton, a drab, a trull

ety: ćheṅć˚ = Prk. छेच्छ(इ) = S. छेत्स्य(ति), fut. of rt. छिद् (used for the pres.)
def: v.t. To mince, back, chop, pound, &c.
s. (dakh.) A hand-ball, foot-ball ( Harris ): see گيند gend.
s.f. = ćheʼonī, q.v.
more: n. f. S. छेदनी from छिद to cut. 1. A chisel. 2. ( Mech .) A wedge.

def: s.f. Detention, stoppage, interruption, restraint, arrest; sequestration, confiscation.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀙𑀺𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀸 (chikkā), from Sanskrit छिक्का (chikkā, onomatopoeia).

def: s.m. Detention, &c. ( = ćhenk, q v.).
more: (from چهينکنا) s. m. Sequestration of goods.

def: v.t. To interrupt, to interfere with, cross the path of; to detain, prevent, stop, retain, bar, restrain, arrest; — to cut out, to cross or score out, to cancel; — to surpass, excel
subs: v.t. (intens.) To stop, &c.; to sequester, to confiscate.

def: s.m. One who detains or stops, &c.; a confiscator.
n. f. H. choṭ . A slight cut or bruise; scrape; scar.
Panj. n. m. S. कीट an insect. A large black ant. Tumhāre peṭ meṅ to chīnṭe kī gāṅṭh hai . Prov. The orifice of your stomach is like an ant's. (You eat so little!)
adj. Sticky; gummy.

s.f. Touching, touch, handling, passing the hand over; meddling with, molesting, molestation, provocation, excitement, irritating, irritation, vexing, vexation, worrying, annoying; — touching up, stirring up, making active; action, activity, stir; incitement, stimulus, fillip; — dalliance, flirtation, amorous intercourse or skirmishing; jest, fun
subs: ćheṛ-khānī, s.f. Touching, touch, &c. ( = ćheṛ); — stricture, animadversion; mutual action or exertion; interchange (of compliments, &c.); attack and counter-attack; — tightening the strings or screws (of an instrument), tuning (an instrument).

def: s.m. A goat (see ćherī).

s.m. Touch; provocation, &c. ( = ćher, q.v.). s.f. Touching, touch, handling, passing the hand over; meddling with, molesting, molestation, provocation, excitement, irritating, irritation, vexing, vexation, worrying, annoying; — touching up, stirring up, making active; action, activity, stir; incitement, stimulus, fillip; — dalliance, flirtation, amorous intercourse or skirmishing; jest, fun
more: (from چهيڙنا q. v.) s. m. Touch, &c.
subs: ćheṛ-khānī, s.f. Touching, touch, &c. ( = ćheṛ); — stricture, animadversion; mutual action or exertion; interchange (of compliments, &c.); attack and counter-attack; — tightening the strings or screws (of an instrument), tuning (an instrument).

def: s. f. Slavery.
s.f. A she-goat.
v.n. To be troubled with indigestion, to have a bad digestion (cf. ćheṛnā).
more: v. a. 1. To touch ( chhūnā ); lay one's hand on. Tum to mujhe chheṛoge! Prov. You 'll lay your hand on me! (I am afraid you are going to kiss me). ☞ The story is that, as a woman with an empty pitcher on her head was passing a man in the street, her modesty took alarm so that she started back a few paces, saying 'Now dont take liberties with me.' 'How could I,' said the man, 'seeing that both hands are occupied with these pigeons.' 'Oh,' said affrighted modesty—'I know you would secure those in my pitcher.' 2. To tickle; pass or run the fingers over. Hāthoṅ upar uchhāle aur chheṛ-kar haṅsāve, Koi chusnī muṅh meṅ deve, koī jhunjhunā bajāve . The love of children. Nazīr. Or dandled in their arms some tickling make them laugh, A stick to suck, or rattles sound in their behalf. 3. To spur; give a fillip; push on. Chaudhrī, zarā chheṛe chalo . Use the goad a little, driver. 4. To joke with; d…
v. a. To have a bad digestion.

ety: ćheṛ˚ = Prk. छेड(इ) or छेडे(इ), fr. S. p.p.p. क्षिप्त
def: v.t. To touch, lay the hand on, pass the hand over; to meddle with, molest, interrupt, disturb, trouble, annoy, tease, torment, worry, irritate, vex, excite, provoke; to touch up, stir up, incite, stimulate, jog, urge, spur; — to question closely, or searchingly, or strictly, to call to account, take to task, censure, rebuke; to address unseemly language to, to abuse, insult; to laugh at, quiz, deride; — to touch upon, to begin; — to tune (an instrument preparatory to playing on it), to strike the chords, to begin playing.
more: v. a. To irritate, vex, excite, fret, trouble, disturb, molest, abuse, insult, aggress, to touch, handle, stir up, interrupt. 2. To play on a musical instrument.

def: n. f. Countenance; appearance; shape; figure. Āp kī cheshṭā to dekho!

n. f. S. चित to think. 1. Care; anxiety; concern. 2. See chintā , (3). 3. Readiness; expertness; diligence. 4. Divine inspiration. cheṭak lagnā , v. n. To think of; feel auxiety about. Ise to uskī cheṭak lagī huī hai .
(caus. of چيتنا) v. a. (dakh.) To light (a fire), to inflame ( Harris ): see چتانا chitānā.

ety: ćheṭ˚ = Prk. छित्त(इ)? fr. S. क्षिप्त p.p.p. rt. क्षिप्; cf. ćhednā
def: v.t. To break, crush, bruise, pound; to beat, hammer, flatten.
more: W. v. a. 1. To beat; flatten; hammer. 1. Chār rupaë ke kaṛe chhet de . 2. Terā mūṅh hī mūṅh chhetūṅ . Wom. I would just like to hammer your mouth. 2. To break clods with a wooden roller. 3. See pīṭnā , 1—4, 6. 4. To earn; make.

def: s.m. Landed property, land, soil, a field ( = khet), plantation; a battle-field; a sphere of action; — place, region, country; a sacred spot or district, a place of pilgrimage (as Benares, &c.), &c. (see kshetr); — a geometrical figure, a plane surface
subs: s.m. A man employed to guard fields from depredation; — proprietor of the soil, landlord, &c. ( = bhūmiyā, q.v.)
s.m. Superficial contents or area (of a field or a plane surface, or of a geometrical figure)
s.f. The boundaries of a field, or of a sacred place.

def: s.m. The owner, or the cultivator of a field, &c., an agriculturist, a husbandman; — husband.
s.m. = ćhatrī, q.v. s.m. The second or military caste of Hindūs; a member of the military or reigning class, a warrior, a soldier.

ety: ćhew+lī = Prk. अडिआ = S. ल or र+इका
def: s.f. The Indian butter-tree, Bassia butyracea.
s.m. A cut, incision; mark, stroke; a piece (of wood, &c.) cut off, a chip
more: n. m. 1. A stroke; cut; mark. 2. A piece of earth, or wood cut off. chheo mārnā , v. n. To line out; mark off.
(see چهيونا) s. m. A mark, a piece of wood cut off. چهيومارنا chheʼo mārnā, To line out, to mark.
subs: (-meṅ or -par), To make an incision (in), to mark, to line out.

def: s.m. A stroke or line (in writing), space, dash, comma, stop; — (dialec.) inoculation, vaccination
more: n. m. 1. A stop; dash. 2. Rus. Vaccination ( ṭīkā ). chhevā denā , v. a. 1. To make a stop or full-stop. 2. Rus . To vaccinate ( ṭīkā lagānā ).
subs: (-ko), To make a line, a dash, or stop, &c.; — to vaccinate.

ety: ćhew+ar = Prk. अडो = S. अ+रः, and ˚ur = Prk. उल्लो = S. ऊलः
def: s.m. Skin, cuticle.

ety: chew˚ = Prk. छेअ (इ), fr. S. छेद, rt. छिद्
def: v.t. To cut, make an incision in, to lance, to mark; to pierce or bleed a tār-tree, to extract tāṛī or palm-juice; to notch (a millstone); — to shake, stir, disturb.
more: v. a. 1. To extract palm juice. 2. To stir; shake; disturb. 3. To apply the lancet; to open.
v. a. 1. To pierce or to bleed a (تاڙ) tāṛ tree, to extract tāṛī (تاڙي). 2. To mark.

def: s.f. An instrument for piercing with; a tool for notching millstones, &c.; a piercer, a chisel, a punch; (Mech.) a wedge.
more: (from چهيونا chheʼonā, q. v.) s. f. A chisel, punch, piercer.

def: n. m. Dim. chhabṛī , n. f. A large basket.
s. f. Buttermilk.
( Math .) Co-tangent.

n. f. S. छुछुन्दरी. 1. The muskrat; Sorex cœrulescens. Is par chhachūṅdar phir gaī hai . 2. A squib (firework). 3. A rover; gadabout. chhachhūṅdar chhoṛnā , v. a. 1. To let off a squib. 2. To make a quarrel; set by the ears.
s. A water-bag.
n. f. S. क्षय to destroy, Pr. jhijjaī . Mortality; destruction; annihilation. Rām kī jaë aur Rāvan kī chhaë! chhaë-rog . Consumption ( tap-i-diq ).
s. f. Ashes.
s. m. A coxcomb, a fop.
s. f. Discoloration.
v. a. (dakh.) To satiate, to satisfy.
(from چهکنا) s. f. Satiety.

adj. An aṅgrakhā , (Sant. aṅgrop ) or Native coat, so called because the front and back are made up of six small pieces. chhamāsṛ'ā , n. m. chhamāsṛī , Wom. n. f. H. chha six, mās month. A boy or girl six months old; a child; a young boy.

n. m. S. शकट a cart; Pr. saaḍhā . 1. A two-wheeled bullock cart; a waggon; a goods' cart. Sau gāṛī na ek chhakṛā, Sau harām-zāde na ek maghrā . Prov. A hundred carriage loads one goods-cart will take in, A hundred rascals dwell one stubborn man within. 2. One who is worn out.
n. f. 1. An aggregate of six. 2. A throw of six (dice).
n. m. A fragrant moss (?)
n. f. The sound of heavy rain, small bells, etc.

n. f. Half-yearly payment, ceremony, etc. Rasm hai murde kī chha-māhī ek, Khalq kā hai isī chalan pe madār, Mujh ko dekho to hūṅ ba-qaid-i-hayāt, Aur chha-māhī ho sāl meṅ do bār! Gālib. But once, the half year closed, the funeral feast is held, Such is the custom 'mongst us mortals yet upheld, But me behold still in the bonds of life appear, My funeral feast I'm made to greet twice in the year. (The Poet Laureate humorously expostulates in verse with the King of Delhī on the delay in the payment of his pension which was twelve months in arrears).
n. m. A kind of verse (S. samassyā ).
s. f. (dakh.) Investigation, search, examination.
(dakh.) To chink, clang ( Harris ).

n. f. S. छाया A shadow ( sāyā ). Bin kāṭe tarvar jathā, miṭe kaun bidhī chhāṅ, Tiaun, Tulsī, updesh bin nahīṅ sansai ko nāh . Tulsī Dās. Until the tree's cut down the shadow will remain, So doubts obscure the mind until you knowledge gain. chhāṅ chhīlnā , Wom. v. a lit . to skin one's shadow. To be one's mortal enemy; to be at daggers drawn.
s. m. Investigation.
n. m. Trickery ( pharphaṅd ).
s. m. Trick, cheating, deceiving.
s. f. 1. Shade, shadow. 2. The reflection of any object in a mirror, &c. (Plur. چهاٴين chhāʼeṅ ). چهانـﮩـ بانـﮩـ chhāṅh-bāṅh, s. f. Auspices.
s. f. Shadiness.
adj. Umbrageous.

n. f. S. छिद Pālī chhindati . 1. Parings; scraps, chips; cuttings; slips; etc. 2. A small bundle of thorns about a foot high. 3. The cut or fashion (of clothes). 4. Extraction; excerption; elimination; selection; pick.

p. adj. 1. Selected; chosen (Sant. bachhāo ); picked. 2. Dissolute; debauched; profligate; rascally. Ajī! yeh Jāme masjid ke chhaṅṭe huë haiṅ . This fellow, my dear sir, is a consummate scoundrel of scoundrels of the Jama Masjid. (Comp. 'Ye have made it (the temple) a den of thieves.')
s. f. 1. Cleaning. 2. The price paid for cleaning.

n. m. 1. (Tir. chhāṅṭal ) Husked rice, etc. 2. Cuttings; scantlings; lees; dross; refuse; pus . 3. The secundines or after-birth. 4. Retrenchments; reductions; savings. 5. A detachment on forage. khafīf chhaṅṭāo , Cheese-parings and candleends.

v. a. 1. To cut; clip; lop off; shear; crop; dock; pare; thin; prune. 2. To cut out (clothes). 3. To clear, drain (land). 4. To make up; concoct; hatch; utter. Kyā baiṭhe bāteṅ chhāṅṭ rahe ho? 5. To cut down; cut short; reduce; abridge; curtail; retrench. 6. To pick; choose; select; pick out. 7. To thresh; separate corn from the husk; sift. 8. To weed; purge; expurgate. 9. To scrub; wash; clean. Mail achchhī tarah chhāṅṭo . 10. To vomit; cast up; disgorge. 11. To reject; discard; dismiss. 12. To kill; slaughter; butcher.
v. n. See chhāṅṭnā , v. a.
v. a. Caus. of چهاتَنا q. v.
s. f. Shade, shadow. See چهانو chhāṅw.
A tax on stamping cloth.
(from چهپانا) s. f. Edition, the price of printing, the act of printing.
n. f. S. षटपदी A poem in Hindi verse, consisting of six hemistichs. Kellogg .
more: s. m. A kind of measure of Hindī verse.

def: n. m. A press; printing office. chhāpe-khāne-wālā , n. m. The proprietor of a press. chhāpne-vālā, chhapaiyā , A printer; a pressman.
s. (dakh. Harris ) See چهپرکهٿ chappar-khaṭ.
s. (dakh. Harris ) See چهپرکهٿ chappar-khaṭ.
s. m. Name of a medicinal herb.
n. m. A mace-bearer.

n. f. 1. Flags over the temple of a god or goddess. 2. Trimmings; lace; stripes. 3. A Hin. Marriage ceremony in which the bride and bridegroom have a duel with sticks covered with flowers on the fourth day after marriage. phūl-chhaṛiyāṅ, phūl kī chhaṛī , n. f. Sticks covered with flowers. 1. Phūl-chhaṛyāṅ na māro, ho bālamā! 2. Maiṅ ne to kabhī iske phūl kī chhaṛī bhī nahīṅ mārī . I never so much as touched him with a spring of flowers.

v. a. S. चट separate. 1. To pound and husk (Sant. huṛuṅg ). Mūsal bin nāj chhaṛūṅ kaise? Okhal phūṭī, mūsal ṭūṭā, sāsaṛ, āj karūṅ kaise? W. Rus. Wom. song. Without a pestle how the rice am I to pound and husk to day? The mortar's cracked, the pestle's broke, I cannot pound, do what I may. 2. To beat; chastise (Sant. charnāo ).
s. (dakh.) A child, infant. چهروادان کاگهر chharwādān kā ghar, A nursery ( Harris ).
adj. (dakh.) Sixty six: see چهيا سٿهـ chhiyāsaṭh.
adj. Waspish, peevish.

n. f. S. षट्टङ्क The sixteenth part of a seer; two ounces. chhaṭankī , n. f. A two-ounce weight. chhaṭaṅkī lagānā , v. a. lit . to disencumber oneself, so as to weigh but two ounces. To show a light pair of heels. Ūh to chhaṭaṅkī lagā gayo, mhāre hāth nāhīṅ āyo . Rus.
n. m. Clearing grain or rice from the husk.
s. m. (dakh.) Liking, desire.
s. (dakh.) Galloping horse ( Harris ).
adj. 1. Rejected; thrown aside or out. 2. Picked out; selected; choice.
s. f. The sixth day of a lunar fortnight.
(dakh.) The nightmare ( Harris ).
v. a. (dakh.) See چهانٿنا chhānṭnā.
v. n. To be agitated.
n. f. 1. Agitation; flurry; restlessness. 2. Hurry; haste.
n. m. Dispersion; scattering; prostration. Oloṅ ne chhatrāo kar dīyā .
s. (dakh.) A weight about two ounces (? Qanoon-e-islam ).
adj. Cunning; crafty; artful.
adj. (dakh.) See خوش باش ḵẖụsh bāsh, under خوش ḵẖụsh.
Hin. n. m. Fixing a date for calling a woman to her husband's or father's house.

n. f. Filth; dirt; excrement. 1. Chhīchhī chhīchhī kavvā khāë, Dūdh malāī nannā khāë . Wom. Nursery song (while washing a child's face). Foul and foul the crow picks up, baby milk and cream licks up. 2. Chhīchhī kar-le . Wom. Go to the pot . (Ease yourself). 3. Chhichhī dhulvāo . Wom. Wash yourself. 4. Gū nahīṅ, chhīchhī . Prov. Not excrement but filth. (Different names for the same thing).
adj. (dakh.) Ninety six: see چهيانوي chhiyānawwe.
adj. (dakh.) Empty, mean: see چهوچها chhūchhā, and چهوچهي chhūchhī, sign 1.
s. f. Shallowness.
adj. (dakh.) See چهچهوڙا chhichhoṛā.
n. m. 1. Childishness; puerility. 2. Shallowness; meanness; baseness, 3. Display; show.

n. m. 1. The cellular membrane that covers the surface of the body; the internal cellular web. 2. Entrails; guts. Haḍḍī na paslī, mūā chhīchṛā hai, Zanānā na mardanā, mūā hījṛā hai . Nor bones nor back this wretch has, mere offal is this thing, Nor man nor woman but a eunuch good for nothing. 3. The slough (of a wound or sore). 4. ( Met .) A brat; bantling. chhichhṛe bakhernā , v. a. See bhor k .
= 3 × 20 + 10 + 6, adj. S. षट्सप्तती Seventy-six.
adj. Seventy six.
adj. (dakh.) Vagabond: see آواره āwāra.
adj. Shattered; shivered; broken in pieces.

n. f. Snatching; taking by force. Chhīn jhapaṭ more hāth se gāgar, jor se baiyāṅ marorī, Dil dhaṛkat hai, sāṅs chaṛhat hai, deṅh kapat gorī gorī, Shām mo se khelo na Horī, pā lāgī kar jorī . Holi. My pitcher snatched from me, he gave my arm a twist, 'My heart beat fast, breath quick, a tremor o'er me came, Dont Holi play with me, Oh spare my too slight frame! Prostrate behold me at your feet, O Sham, but list!'
(caus. of چهينکنا chhenknā ) v. a. To cause to stop, cause to seize, to cancel, strike out.

n. f. 1. Sprinklings or drops of water. 1. Dekho kisī par chhīṅṭ na paṛe . 2. Na īṅṭ ḍālo, na chhīṅṭoṅ bharo . Prov. Nor throw a stone, nor get splashed. 2. A speck; spot; stain; blot ( dhabbā ). 1. Siyāhī kī chhīṅṭ paṛ gaī . 2. Chhīṅṭ ughaṛ āī hai . Wom. She is paying for her sins in a former birth. 3. Chintz. 4. Balance; remainder; the odd days of a month. 5. A very quarrelsome or wicked person. chhīṅṭ kī kauṛī , n. f. Wages for the odd days, or for any period less than a month.

n. m. 1. A splash; sprinkling. 2. A shower; light rain. Ek chhīṅṭā ho gayā . 3. Scattering; sowing broad-cast. 4. A lump of tobacco or opium. Chanḍū ke do chhīṅṭe uṛāë . 5. Stimulus; excitement ( baṛhāvā ). 6. Chaff; joke; jesting. Do chhīṅṭoṅ meṅ ro dīyā . 7. Deception; cheat; trick. Kis ke chhīṅṭoṅ meṅ ā gaë? 8. Sowing an inferior crop between the lines of the chief crop. 9. Seed scattered at the extremities of a village with a view to secure possession. chhīṅṭā denā yā mārnā , v. a. 1. To sprinkle; splash. 2. To sprinkle water over a person just recovered from small-pox. 3. To cheat; play a trick upon. 4. To pat on the shoulder; to cheer. chhāṅṭam chhāṅṭā , n. m. Splashing one another.
v. a. To sprinkle; scatter.
(from چهپانا chhipānā ) s. m. Concealment.
n. f. pl. chhipṭiyāṅ . (H. chhīlan ; Sant. chhailā ) Chippings; chips; shavings; splinters. chhipṭī , adj. Thin; delicate. Chhipṭī sī aurat hai .
Caus. of chhiṛaknā , q. v.
n. m. S. शीक to sprinkle. Sprinkling; watering.
s. m. Sprinkling, watering.
wety: From چھڑک (chiṛak) + ـاؤ (-āo, verbal noun suffix).

def: v. n. See chheṛ'nā , v. a.
Caus. of chheṛnā , q. v.
v. n. 1. See bikharnā , 1—4. 2. To shine; to be bright. Chāṅdnī chhiṭak rahī hai . 3. To be displaced, dislocated. Fote chhiṭak gaë .

v. a. S. च्युतकृ from च्युत fallen, कृ to do. 1. To scatter ( chhitrānā ). Sir ke kes daë chhiṭkāë, Mutyan māṅg laī bharvāë . Chaupaī. Her hair loose on her shoulders hung, Pearls down the middle part bright strung. 2. To displace; derange ( utārnā , 17); put out of joint; dislocate.
v. a. (dakh.) To sprinkle, besprinkle, asperse ( Harris ).
adj. S. षक्षवति Pālī, chhannavutī . Ninety-six.
adj. S. षट्षष्टि Sixty-six.
adj. Sixty six.
v. a. To fondle.
s. m. Release, omission, leaving, sparing, letting go.
s. f. Smallness.
s. m. (dakh.) Littleness, minority; childhood.
n. f. The sound made by washermen beating clothes against a stone. Baṛī chhuāchhū ho rahī hai .

n. f. The refuse left after pressing the juice of sugar-cane, etc. chhūchhā, chhūchhī , adj. 1 (Tir. chhuchh ) Only; sole; pure. Bāssā tau chhūchā ghī pīvaë saë . Rus. The king lives on butter. (The rustic's idea of supreme felicity). 2. Empty ( khālī ); hollow. Bharlī hāṅdī bharī pūrī, Chhūṅchhī hāṅdī bāje ṭan ṭan . Bhoj. Prov. A vessel full gives forth no sound, The empty vessel doth resound. 3. Contemptible; despicable; shallow. chhūch uṛānā , v. a. See bhus uṛānā , 1.
s. (dakh.) A sort of squib: see چهچهوندر chhuchhūndar, sign. 2.
n. m. Blame ( ilzām ); calumny.
v. a. To whitewash.
s. m. A date. (Phœnix dactylifera).
s. f. Touch.
s. f. Chalk.
n. f. S. छुपन्मृता. 1. The sensitive plant; mimosa pudica . 2. Any thing very delicate or frail. 3. A touchy, testy, sensitive, or irritable person.
n. f. Babble; murmur.

v. a. 1. To make a rippling sound; babble; murmur. 2. To come off (as loose fœces ) with a rumbling interrupted noise. 3. To make water slowly (as a mare, etc. in heat). chhul chhul karte phir'nā , v. n. To flit about restlessly, now here, now there.
adj. Boyish, puerile.
n. f. H. chhoṭā little, unglī finger. The little finger.
s. f. Liberation.
n. f. Ransom.
adj. chhū touch. Defiled by touching; polluted (applied to drinking vessels, dishes, etc.); impure; unclean.
more: adj. Defiled by touching, foul, corrupt, polluted (applied to vessels, dishes, &c.).

def: v. a. To cause to be separated. See چهرَانا Dūdh chhuṭā diyā . The child has been weaned.
adj. (dakh.) Being let go, being shot forth.
n. f. Remission of revenue.

n. m. H. chhuṭnā to be freed. 1. Liberation; disengagement; release; discharge; acquittal; deliverance; liberty; exemption. 2. Leisure; spare time; time. Ghar se zarā chhuṭkārā nahīṅ hotā. chhuṭkārā pānā yā h. , v. n. To be freed, released; to get rid of. chhuṭkārā denā , v. a. To set free; let off; release; acquit; discharge from liability.
v. n. See chhuṭnā. Tum rūṭhe, ham chhūṭe . Prov. You are angry, I am free.
intj. Fy! foh! faugh! — ćhī-ćhī, s.f. Dirt, filth, excrement; — (colloq.) an epithet applied to a class of Eurasians (by whom the expression ćhī is commonly used); — intj. Fy! shame! &c. (see ćhī).
n. m. Pudendum muliebre .
adj. (f. -ī), Shallow, of little depth.
s.f. Shallowness.
s.f. The play of ducks and drakes
more: s. f. The play of ducks and drakes. چهچهلي کهيلنا chhichhlī khelnā, To play at ducks and drakes.
s. f. The play of a dog or cat with game, &c.
subs: To play at ducks and drakes.

ety: Prk. छुच्छअडओ (with w inserted); S. तुच्छक+र+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Light, vain, airy, petty, trifling, frivolous, puerile, childish, shallow, superficial; tawdry, showy, ostentatious; low, mean, insignificant, contemptible.
adj. = ćhićhorā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Light, vain, airy, petty, trifling, frivolous, puerile, childish, shallow, superficial; tawdry, showy, ostentatious; low, mean, insignificant, contemptible.
s.m. Lightness, airiness, frivolity, puerility; shallowness; meanness; show, display, &c.
s.m. = ćhićṛā, q.v. s.m. Skin, cuticle, integument, the cellular membrane that covers the surface of the body; slough (of a wound), crust (of a sore); the prepuce, foreskin; entrails; offal.
s.m. Skin, membrane, &c. ( = ćhićṛā, q.v.); the skinny part of meat, tough and uneatable part of flesh
more: s. m. The skinny and rejected part of meat, a piece of flesh.
subs: To flay; to tear to pieces.

def: v. n. To scrawl, to score.
adj. (f. -ī), ćhićhorā, q.v.
more: adj. H. chhī pooh, chhorā boy. 1. Childish; shallow; light; trifling; mean; base. 2. Showy; ostentatious.

def: s.m. Skin, cuticle, integument, the cellular membrane that covers the surface of the body; slough (of a wound), crust (of a sore); the prepuce, foreskin; entrails; offal.
more: s. m. Sink, slough (of a wound), skin.

def: adj. Skinny, lean.
adj. Thin, not close (as cloth, &c.), sparse, straggling, widely separated, &c. ( = ćhidrā, q.v.).
more: adj. Thin; not close. See chhidrā .
adj. Thin, not close (said of a person or animal whose legs are much separated).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Pierced, bored, perforated.
v.t. To cause to pierce; to perforate; to get pierced or perforated ( = ćhidwānā).
more: v. a. Caus. of چهيدنا q. v.
(causal of چهيدنا chhednā ) v. a. To cause to pierce, to perforate.

def: v.n. To be pierced, to be bored
adj. (f. -ī), Containing gaps or openings, thin (as cloth, a crop, &c.), not closely woven; sparse, meagre; straggling, widely separated, wide apart (as the legs of a person or animal; = ćhīdā).
more: adj. Opp. of ghankā . Thin (cloth, crop); spare; meagre; not close; straggling. Kaisā chhidrā goṭā bunā hai .

def: s.m. A hole, orifice, opening, aperture, a crack; a defect, fault, flaw; weakness, weak point; — adj. Pierced, perforated (see ćhidrā; chīdā).
more: n. m. S. छिद्र A hole ( chhed ).

def: v.t. To place at wide intervals, to spread out; — v.n. To walk with the legs far apart; — ćhidrā-kar ćalnā, v.n. Idem.
more: v. a. To spread out; to place at wide intervals. chhidrā-kar chalnā, chhidrānā , v. n. To walk with one's legs spread out, or with difficulty.

def: s.f. = S چهدرتو छिद्रत्व ćhidratva, s.m. The state of being perforated, the quality of giving space, holes, or openings; the quality of being pervaded by everything.
s.m. = S چهدرتا छिद्रता ćhidratā, s.f. The state of being perforated, the quality of giving space, holes, or openings; the quality of being pervaded by everything.
part. Pierced, perforated, having holes, gaps, or openings.
v.t. To cause to be pierced, to havo or get pierced or bored.
s. Name of a city of Turkistan, the people of which are extremely handsome, and unequalled in archery: clay, mud.
v. a. To embellish, to decorate.
adj. Seventy-six.
s. m. 1. Mud, ooze, slime, mire. 2. A splinter of wood. چـﮩ‍لا نکلنا chihlā nikalnā, To fatigue, to be tired. چـﮩ‍لا کرنا chihlā-karnā, To split to pieces.
more: s. m. A large wooden peg, a tent-pin.

def: (from چـﮩ‍لا chihlā ) adj. Splashy, muddy, slimy.
s. m. See چـنـﮩـ chinh.
(or, dakh. chahr ) s. Face: see چـﮩره chihra.
s.f. Loss, waste, wane, diminution, ebb (of tide, &c.); destruction, decay, wasting or wearing away; — ćhīj-baṭṭā, ćhīj-ćhapaṭ, s.m. Tare and tret.
more: n. f. S. क्षय from क्षि to wane. 1. Decrease; diminution; wane; ebb; decay. 2. Waste; wear and tear; loss; damage. Man meṅ ser kī chhīj hai. chhīj baṭṭā, chīj chhapaṭ , n. m. Tare and tret.

def: v.t. To cause to waste or wear away; to decrease, to lessen.

ety: ćhīj˚ = Prk. छिज्ज(इ) = S. छिद्य(ते), pass. fr. rt. छिद्
def: v.n. To decrease, dwindle, diminish, wane; to decay, waste away, fade, wither, pine away, become emaciated, to wear away (by attrition, &c.), to be effaced or removed
more: v. n. 1. To decrease ( ghaṭnā ); dwindle; wane; decay. Banaj kare to ṭoṭā āë, baiṭh khāë dhan chhīje, Kaheṅ Kabīr, suno bhai sādhoṅ, māṅg khāë so jīte . Kabīr. Who trade must suffer loss, who sit at home their wealth must drain, Kabīr hear, brother saint! who begs and eats his is the gain. 2. To shrivel; lose flesh; waste; pine away. 3. To be effaced or removed (evil); to be affected by; to receive impressions. Pānī meṅ pākhān, bhīje, par chhīje nahīṅ; Mūrakh āge gyān, rījhe, par būjhe nahīṅ . Prov. As stone in water's wet, but no impression takes, So laughs the fool at words, their meaning he mistakes. 4. To die; expire ( utarnā , 32). Bachcha chhīj gayā .

v. n. To decrease, to pine away. ديکها ديکهي کيجي جوگ چهيجي کايا باڙهي روگ dekhā dekhī kīje jog, chhīje kāyā bāṛhe rog, They who merely imitate others in austerity, waste away, and their diseases increase.

def: s.m. = ćhīṅkā, q.v. s.m. A network of strings or cords to hang anything in (as food, pots, a burthen at either end of a yoke or pole, &c.); the cords of a bahangī, q.v.; a rope-bridge across a hill-torrent, or a rope with a sling or basket attached (by which travellers are drawn across); — a muzzle (for cattle).
more: s. m. A sling for carrying baskets, &c. See چهينکا chhīnkā.

def: n. m. S. चिक्कणत्व greasiness. The dirt which settles on a lamp. chīkaṭ, chikkaṭ , adj. Oily; greasy; dirty; soiled.
